description: A place giving temporary protection from bad weather or danger; to provide such protection. 'Shuroa' names the protective space—roof over head, walls against wind, safety from exposure.
sound_symbolism: The word covers and protects. 'Shu' /ʃu/ begins with soft enclosure, protection forming. 'Ro' surrounds steadily. Finally 'a' opens to safety within, shelter complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun or verb. As noun: 'mia mua shuroa thalu.' (I stay in the shelter.) As verb: 'mia thia shuroa.' (I shelter you.) Related to 'womu' (home) but emphasizes protection rather than belonging; joins 'shurano' (safe) and 'shorupo' (protect) in the safety family.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Shelter enables other needs—without safety and protection, higher concerns cannot be addressed.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Shelter was first necessity—protection from elements before all other concerns.
